overview: The PLI Scheme for Textiles was notified on 24/09/2021 to promote production of MMF Apparel & Fabrics and Technical Textiles products in India, enabling the textile industry to achieve size and scale, become globally competitive, create employment opportunities, and support viable enterprises. The scheme has an approved outlay of Rs. 10,683 crore and aims to drive fresh investment of over Rs. 19,000 crore and additional production turnover of over Rs. 3 lakh crore over five years. It includes two investment tracks: Part-1 requiring minimum ₹300 Crore investment and Part-2 requiring minimum ₹100 Crore investment, with incentives linked to turnover achievement.
objectives: Promote production of MMF Apparel & Fabrics and Technical Textiles products in the country
Enable textile industry to achieve size and scale and become globally competitive
Support creation of a viable enterprise and competitive textile industry
Drive fresh investment of above Rs 19,000 crore over five years
Achieve additional production turnover of over Rs. 3 lakh crore in five years
Prioritize investment in Aspirational Districts & Tier 3/4 towns
Positively impact States like Gujarat, UP, Maharashtra, Tamil Nadu, Punjab, AP, Telangana, Odisha
eligibility: Any person including Company/Firm/LLP/Trust willing to create a separate manufacturing company under Companies Act 2013, and invest minimum 300 Crore (excluding land and administrative building cost) to manufacture Notified Products for Scheme Part-1, or minimum 100 Crore for Scheme Part-2. The applicant must form a new/separate company under Companies Act, 2013 before commencement of investment, and the new entity will be known as Participant. Investment made in anticipation from the date of Scheme Notification shall also be accounted for calculation of threshold investment provided their proposal is approved for participation under the Scheme.
benefits: Financial incentives linked to turnover achievement, with rates varying by year and scheme part (e.g., 15% for Part-1 Year 1, 11% for Part-2 Year 1). Incentives are payable for a period of 5 years only from the first performance year. Participants are eligible to apply for other applicable Government of India or State Government schemes (e.g., duty remission, duty exemptions, duty drawback). Foreign investment must comply with Consolidated FDI Policy 2020. Participants must commit to setting up manufacturing facilities with appropriate quality and testing facilities conforming to BIS or International Standards, and ensure 'Made in India' tags on products.
financial_support: Incentives are calculated on achieving threshold turnover as mentioned in para 4.1/4.2 under the respective scheme in the first Performance Year. Rate of incentives for a particular performance year are as indicated in Table 2: For Scheme Part-1: Year 1 (FY 2024-2025) - 600 Cr turnover at 15% rate; Year 2 - 750 Cr at 14%; Year 3 - 937.5 Cr at 13%; Year 4 - 1171.87 Cr at 12%; Year 5 - 1464.84 Cr at 11%. For Scheme Part-2: Year 1 - 200 Cr turnover at 11% rate; Year 2 - 250 Cr at 10%; Year 3 - 312.5 Cr at 9%; Year 4 - 390.63 Cr at 8%; Year 5 - 488.2 Cr at 7%. There is a provision of cap of 10% over and above the prescribed minimum incremental turnover growth of 25% for the purpose of calculation of incentives from Year 2 onward. For Year 1, the cap of 10% is applied over and above turnover of two times of the investment made under the Scheme up to 2024-25.
application_process: 1. Register on the Ministry’s PLI portal developed for this purpose in the prescribed format. 2. Submit application with required documents. 3. Accept Undertaking and Pay application fee. 4. Upload required documents (as per Type of applicant). 5. E-Sign uploaded documents via OTP validation form Aadhaar. 6. Final Submit.
deadlines: Rolling basis — applications are accepted year-round with Weekly Open House Forum every Friday between 4PM to 5PM to understand and resolve issues faced by participants.
implementing_agency: Ministry of Textiles, Government of India
caveats: Turnover achieved from trading and job work will not be accounted for incentives under the Scheme.
Notified goods/Products manufactured by a company other than the registered participant company, even of same group, shall not be accounted for calculation of incremental turnover.
Only Notified Products sold under GST Invoice indicating 8 digit HS code shall be taken into account for calculation of threshold/incremental turnover.
If multiple applications are filed by the same group companies, only one project under PLI will be approved for any group of companies.

The Participant shall declare the Notified ITC HS lines/Technical Textiles Products as applicable they shall manufacture in the new participant company, and lines/products once selected should be adhered to till Scheme duration; any changes require prior approval of MoT.
Incentive will be available only for a period of 5 years only from the first performance year.

In case the prescribed conditions are not met in time, the incentive will be available from the year these are met, for a lesser number of years but rate of incentive applicable will be as prescribed to first year of the scheme and so on for remaining period.
There will be no restriction for making higher investment for enhancing manufacturing capabilities and achieving growth target.

Participant should commit for setting up of manufacturing facilities to manufacture Notified Product(s) along with appropriate quality and testing facilities having conformity to prescribed BIS Standards or any other International Standard.
The Participant, while dispatching Notified Products out of factory, shall ensure to put 'Made in India' tag on each product.

The Applicant and its group company(ies) should neither have been declared as bankrupt or defaulter or reported any fraud by any bank or financial institution or non-banking financial company or placed under black list/denied entity list by any state or central Government department/agencies.
source_cited_notes: https://pli.texmin.gov.in/frontend/images/Documents/Announcement of PLI-Textile for MMF and Technical Textile Sector.pdf
